stories.title: Streamline User Requirements Capture from Teams Calls
stories.i_want: a streamlined process for capturing user requirements during Teams calls
stories.so_that: I can ensure all nuances and acceptance criteria are documented accurately without duplicating work
stories.narrative: As a User, I often find that the current process for capturing user requirements during Teams calls is inefficient and prone to errors. Manual copying of notes leads to the loss of important nuances, and I frequently have to transfer information across multiple formats, which results in repetitive work. Additionally, I often miss critical acceptance criteria due to incomplete documentation. By implementing a more efficient system for capturing these requirements, I can improve the accuracy of our documentation, save time on manual data entry, and enhance the clarity of acceptance criteria, ultimately leading to better project outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: A system that automatically captures and organizes user requirements during Teams calls
The ability to document nuances and acceptance criteria directly within the tool
Reduction in time spent on manual data entry by at least 50%
A clear and structured format for documenting requirements that can be easily shared with the Development Team and Project Manager
stories.business_value: Improving the efficiency and accuracy of capturing user requirements will lead to better project alignment, reduced rework, and ultimately faster delivery of features that meet user needs.

rows.story_title: Streamline User Requirements Capture from Teams Calls
rows.task_title: Research and Select Requirements Capture Tool
rows.gherkin:
Given I am a user
When I research available tools
Then I should have a list of tools that meet the requirements
rows.acceptance_criteria:
A list of at least 3 potential tools is created
Each tool includes pros and cons
Integration capabilities with Teams are evaluated
rows.business_value: Improving the efficiency and accuracy of capturing user requirements will lead to better project alignment, reduced rework, and ultimately faster delivery of features that meet user needs.
rows.source_excerpt:
Interviewer: Can you walk me through the pain points in capturing user requirements?
User: Notes from Teams calls are manually copied into backlog items and spreadsheets. We lose nuance, repeat work, and miss acceptance criteria.

rows.story_title: Streamline User Requirements Capture from Teams Calls
rows.gherkin:
Given the selected tool is available
When I integrate it with Teams
Then it should capture audio and transcribe notes during calls
rows.acceptance_criteria:
The tool is successfully integrated with Teams
Audio from Teams calls is captured and transcribed accurately
rows.business_value: Improving the efficiency and accuracy of capturing user requirements will lead to better project alignment, reduced rework, and ultimately faster delivery of features that meet user needs.

rows.story_title: Streamline User Requirements Capture from Teams Calls
rows.task_title: Develop User Interface for Documenting Requirements
rows.gherkin:
Given the tool is integrated with Teams
When I access the user interface
Then I should be able to document nuances and acceptance criteria easily
rows.acceptance_criteria:
The interface is intuitive and easy to navigate
Users can document nuances and acceptance criteria without confusion
rows.business_value: Improving the efficiency and accuracy of capturing user requirements will lead to better project alignment, reduced rework, and ultimately faster delivery of features that meet user needs.

rows.story_title: Streamline User Requirements Capture from Teams Calls
rows.task_title: Implement Data Storage for Captured Requirements
rows.gherkin:

When I save the requirements
Then they should be stored securely and be retrievable later
rows.acceptance_criteria:
Requirements are stored securely
Users can retrieve their documented requirements easily
rows.business_value: Improving the efficiency and accuracy of capturing user requirements will lead to better project alignment, reduced rework, and ultimately faster delivery of features that meet user needs.
